changes.value:
## What

Virtual tags need to exist as tag entities (with `virtual: true`) so they show up in tag listings and have proper colors. They should be auto-created/ensured when a board is opened, using metadata from the `VirtualTagRegistry`.

**Files to modify:**
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/tag/add.rs` — add `ensure_virtual_tags(ctx, registry)` function that:
  1. Iterates all strategies in the registry
  2. For each, checks if a tag entity with that slug already exists
  3. If not, creates one with `virtual: true`, the strategy's color and description
  4. If exists but `virtual` is false, sets it to true (idempotent upgrade)
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` or wherever board open happens — call `ensure_virtual_tags` during board initialization

The function should be idempotent — safe to call on every board open.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ensure_virtual_tags()` creates missing virtual tag entities
- [ ] Virtual tags have `virtual: true`, correct color, correct description
- [ ] Existing virtual tags are not duplicated on re-open
- [ ] Non-virtual tags with same name are upgraded to virtual (edge case protection)
- [ ] Function is called during board initialization

## Tests
- [ ] Integration test: open board, verify virtual tag entities exist with correct metadata
- [ ] Integration test: re-open board, verify no duplicate tags created
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

#virtual-tags
VT-3: Auto-create virtual tag entities on board open

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,28 +1,41 @@
 ## What

-Virtual tags need to exist as tag entities (with `virtual: true`) so they show up in tag listings and have proper colors. They should be auto-created/ensured when a board is opened, using metadata from the `VirtualTagRegistry`.
+With Option B, virtual tags are NOT tag entities. They live in the `VirtualTagRegistry` as pure metadata (slug, color, description). The frontend needs this metadata to render virtual tag pills with correct colors and tooltips.
+
+Expose virtual tag metadata through a dedicated API — either a new command or included in an existing response.
+
+### Approach: include in board metadata
+
+The simplest path — add a `virtual_tags` array to the `get board` response (or a new `list virtual_tags` command). Each entry has `slug`, `color`, `description`. The frontend stores this alongside (but separate from) the entity store.

 **Files to modify:**
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/tag/add.rs` — add `ensure_virtual_tags(ctx, registry)` function that:
-  1. Iterates all strategies in the registry
-  2. For each, checks if a tag entity with that slug already exists
-  3. If not, creates one with `virtual: true`, the strategy's color and description
-  4. If exists but `virtual` is false, sets it to true (idempotent upgrade)
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` or wherever board open happens — call `ensure_virtual_tags` during board initialization
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/get.rs` — include `virtual_tags` metadata from the registry in the board response
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/schema-context.tsx` (or a new context) — parse and store virtual tag metadata from board response
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — when resolving a tag entity fails (not in entity store), fall back to virtual tag metadata map for color/description

-The function should be idempotent — safe to call on every board open.
+### Frontend resolution order in MentionPill
+1. Look up slug in entity store (existing behavior — finds real tags)
+2. If not found, look up slug in virtual tag metadata map
+3. If not found, fall back to gray (existing behavior)

+This means NO changes to the entity store, entity YAML, or tag CRUD commands. Virtual tags simply don't exist in the entity system.
+
+**No `ensure_virtual_tags` function needed.** No entity files created. No collision risk.
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `ensure_virtual_tags()` creates missing virtual tag entities
-- [ ] Virtual tags have `virtual: true`, correct color, correct description
-- [ ] Existing virtual tags are not duplicated on re-open
-- [ ] Non-virtual tags with same name are upgraded to virtual (edge case protection)
-- [ ] Function is called during board initialization
+- [ ] `get board` (or dedicated command) returns virtual tag metadata: `[{slug, color, description}]`
+- [ ] Frontend stores virtual tag metadata separately from entity store
+- [ ] `MentionPill` resolves virtual tags via fallback to metadata map
+- [ ] Virtual tag pills render with correct color and description tooltip
+- [ ] No tag entity files created for virtual tags
+- [ ] No name collision possible with user tags

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test: open board, verify virtual tag entities exist with correct metadata
-- [ ] Integration test: re-open board, verify no duplicate tags created
+- [ ] Backend: `get board` includes `virtual_tags` array with correct metadata
+- [ ] Frontend: `MentionPill` renders virtual tag with correct color when not in entity store
+- [ ] Frontend: regular tags still resolve from entity store (no regression)
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
+- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,41 +1,59 @@
 ## What

-With Option B, virtual tags are NOT tag entities. They live in the `VirtualTagRegistry` as pure metadata (slug, color, description). The frontend needs this metadata to render virtual tag pills with correct colors and tooltips.
+The frontend needs virtual tag metadata (slug, color, description, commands) to render pills and wire context menus. Expose this through the `get board` response.

-Expose virtual tag metadata through a dedicated API — either a new command or included in an existing response.
+### Backend: include virtual_tags in board response

-### Approach: include in board metadata
+**Files to modify:**
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/get.rs` — call `registry.metadata()` and include in board response as `virtual_tags` array:
+  ```json
+  {
+    "virtual_tags": [
+      {
+        "slug": "READY",
+        "color": "0e8a16",
+        "description": "Task has no unmet dependencies",
+        "commands": [
+          { "id": "vtag.ready.start", "name": "Start Working", "context_menu": true }
+        ]
+      }
+    ]
+  }
+  ```
+- The board command handler needs access to the `VirtualTagRegistry`. Thread it through `KanbanContext` or pass as a parameter.

-The simplest path — add a `virtual_tags` array to the `get board` response (or a new `list virtual_tags` command). Each entry has `slug`, `color`, `description`. The frontend stores this alongside (but separate from) the entity store.
+### Frontend: store and provide virtual tag metadata

 **Files to modify:**
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/get.rs` — include `virtual_tags` metadata from the registry in the board response
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/schema-context.tsx` (or a new context) — parse and store virtual tag metadata from board response
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — when resolving a tag entity fails (not in entity store), fall back to virtual tag metadata map for color/description
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/schema-context.tsx` (or a new `virtual-tag-context.tsx`) — parse `virtual_tags` from board response, provide via React context:
+  ```ts
+  interface VirtualTagMeta {
+    slug: string;
+    color: string;
+    description: string;
+    commands: Array<{ id: string; name: string; context_menu: boolean; keys?: KeyBindings }>;
+  }
+  // Map<slug, VirtualTagMeta>
+  ```
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — consume virtual tag context for resolution fallback (VT-9 wires the visual distinction, VT-10 wires the commands)

 ### Frontend resolution order in MentionPill
-1. Look up slug in entity store (existing behavior — finds real tags)
-2. If not found, look up slug in virtual tag metadata map
-3. If not found, fall back to gray (existing behavior)
-
-This means NO changes to the entity store, entity YAML, or tag CRUD commands. Virtual tags simply don't exist in the entity system.
-
-**No `ensure_virtual_tags` function needed.** No entity files created. No collision risk.
+1. Look up slug in entity store (existing — finds real tags)
+2. If not found, look up slug in virtual tag metadata map → `isVirtual = true`
+3. If not found, fall back to gray (existing)

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `get board` (or dedicated command) returns virtual tag metadata: `[{slug, color, description}]`
-- [ ] Frontend stores virtual tag metadata separately from entity store
+- [ ] `get board` response includes `virtual_tags` array with slug/color/description/commands
+- [ ] Frontend context provides `Map<string, VirtualTagMeta>` from board response
 - [ ] `MentionPill` resolves virtual tags via fallback to metadata map
 - [ ] Virtual tag pills render with correct color and description tooltip
-- [ ] No tag entity files created for virtual tags
-- [ ] No name collision possible with user tags
+- [ ] Commands from metadata are available to downstream consumers (VT-10)

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Backend: `get board` includes `virtual_tags` array with correct metadata
+- [ ] Backend: `get board` includes `virtual_tags` array with correct metadata and commands
+- [ ] Frontend: virtual tag context provides metadata after board load
 - [ ] Frontend: `MentionPill` renders virtual tag with correct color when not in entity store
-- [ ] Frontend: regular tags still resolve from entity store (no regression)
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
-- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` pass

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

changes.old_value:
## What

The frontend needs virtual tag metadata (slug, color, description, commands) to render pills and wire context menus. Expose this through the `get board` response.

### Backend: include virtual_tags in board response

**Files to modify:**
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/get.rs` — call `registry.metadata()` and include in board response as `virtual_tags` array:
  ```json
  {
    "virtual_tags": [
      {
        "slug": "READY",
        "color": "0e8a16",
        "description": "Task has no unmet dependencies",
        "commands": [
          { "id": "vtag.ready.start", "name": "Start Working", "context_menu": true }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
  ```
- The board command handler needs access to the `VirtualTagRegistry`. Thread it through `KanbanContext` or pass as a parameter.

### Frontend: store and provide virtual tag metadata

**Files to modify:**
- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/schema-context.tsx` (or a new `virtual-tag-context.tsx`) — parse `virtual_tags` from board response, provide via React context:
  ```ts
  interface VirtualTagMeta {
    slug: string;
    color: string;
    description: string;
    commands: Array<{ id: string; name: string; context_menu: boolean; keys?: KeyBindings }>;
  }
  // Map<slug, VirtualTagMeta>
  ```
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — consume virtual tag context for resolution fallback (VT-9 wires the visual distinction, VT-10 wires the commands)

### Frontend resolution order in MentionPill
1. Look up slug in entity store (existing — finds real tags)
2. If not found, look up slug in virtual tag metadata map → `isVirtual = true`
3. If not found, fall back to gray (existing)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `get board` response includes `virtual_tags` array with slug/color/description/commands
- [ ] Frontend context provides `Map<string, VirtualTagMeta>` from board response
- [ ] `MentionPill` resolves virtual tags via fallback to metadata map
- [ ] Virtual tag pills render with correct color and description tooltip
- [ ] Commands from metadata are available to downstream consumers (VT-10)

## Tests
- [ ] Backend: `get board` includes `virtual_tags` array with correct metadata and commands
- [ ] Frontend: virtual tag context provides metadata after board load
- [ ] Frontend: `MentionPill` renders virtual tag with correct color when not in entity store
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` pass

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

#virtual-tags
VT-3: Expose virtual tag metadata + commands via API
